reasoning:
The most recent closing price for HIG on Feb 27, 2026 is 140.23 (very close to the cutoff date price). A time series model gives P(>140.23) = 0.50 based on short-term linear trend, but a seasonal model from previous years brings this down to a combined estimate of 0.41. 

More importantly, looking at recent news, HIG declared a $0.60 per share quarterly dividend on February 18, 2026, with a record date of March 2, 2026. This means the ex-dividend date is exactly on Monday, March 2, 2026. Because stock prices drop by the dividend amount on the ex-dividend date (and March 1 is a Sunday, effectively making Feb 27 or Feb 28 the last cum-dividend close), the stock will mechanically lose $0.60 in value on March 2. The closing price on March 1 resolves to the prior market close (Feb 27: 140.23). The gap is small (0.43%), but it negatively skews the probability of being strictly higher than 140.23. 
Therefore, for the March 8 horizon (1 week out), this mechanical $0.60 drop decreases the baseline ~0.50 probability down to slightly below 0.50 (in the ~0.45-0.48 range). By March 31, normal drift may partially recover this, pushing closer to 0.49, but the early March ex-dividend directly impairs the likelihood of the price exceeding the March 1 close by these target dates.
